input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Shell app has these problems:
1. GoToAsync("productdetails") throws an exception
2. GoToAsync("//products/active") navigates but nothing visible changes
3. My app takes 3 seconds to start and I have 12 pages in Shell
What's likely wrong in each case and how do I fix them?

data.content:
Here are the likely causes and fixes:

**1. `GoToAsync("productdetails")` throws an exception**

The route isn't registered. Non-visual (detail) routes must be explicitly registered:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs or MauiProgram.cs
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));

// Then navigate with:
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails");
```

Only routes defined declaratively in your `Shell` XAML (via `ShellContent`, `Tab`, `FlyoutItem`) are known automatically. Push-style pages need `Routing.RegisterRoute`.

---

**2. `GoToAsync("//products/active")` navigates but nothing renders**

The absolute route (`//`) switches the visible `ShellContent`, but if the target `ShellContent` doesn't define its `ContentTemplate` or `Content`, you get a blank page. Common cause: mismatched `Route` properties in XAML.

```xml
<!-- Verify your Shell hierarchy matches the route segments exactly -->
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <Tab Route="active">
        <ShellContent
            Route="list"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActiveProductsPage}" />
    </Tab>
</FlyoutItem>
```

With this hierarchy the correct route is `//products/active/list`. If you want `//products/active` to work, collapse it:

```xml
<FlyoutItem Route="products">
    <ShellContent Route="active"
        ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:ActiveProductsPage}" />
</FlyoutItem>
```

Debug tip: inspect `Shell.Current.Items` at runtime or enable Shell navigation logging to see the actual registered routes.

---

**3. Slow startup with 12 pages**

Shell instantiates every page whose content is set directly. Use **`ContentTemplate`** (deferred rendering) instead of **`Content`** so pages are created only when navigated to:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Slow — page created immediately -->
<ShellContent Route="settings">
    <local:SettingsPage />
</ShellContent>

<!-- ✅ Fast — page created on first navigation -->
<ShellContent Route="settings"
    ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate local:SettingsPage}" />
```

Apply `ContentTemplate` to all 12 `ShellContent` entries. This is the single biggest Shell startup optimization.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about three common .NET MAUI Shell navigation issues. These are well-known patterns — I can answer directly from knowledge without needing to search a codebase.
